title: The Chomsky Hierarchy
description: A visual representation of the four types of formal grammars in the Chomsky hierarchy, their corresponding automata, and the languages they generate, ordered by increasing restrictive power.
relationships: Each level is a proper subset of the level above it (e.g., Regular ⊂ Context-Free ⊂ Context-Sensitive ⊂ Recursively Enumerable)
Each grammar type is recognized by a specific automaton: Turing Machine (Type 0), Linear-Bounded Automaton (Type 1), Pushdown Automaton (Type 2), Finite Automaton (Type 3)
